description: a4/ workspace hooks. Most logic lives in the Python dispatcher `scripts/a4_hook.py` (subcommands: post-edit, stop, session-start). Two small bash hooks (`cleanup-edited-a4.sh`, `sweep-old-edited-a4.sh`) handle trivial tmp-file lifecycle. Session-scoped edit tracking at `.claude/tmp/a4-edited/a4-edited-<session_id>.txt` lets Stop validate only this session's edits. SessionStart refreshes UC `implemented_by:` reverse-links (reconciliation on permanent state) and reports workspace-wide status-consistency. PostToolUse records edits and reports per-component status-consistency. Design principles — language choice, lifecycle symmetry, ordering, non-blocking, output channels — live in `references/hook-conventions.md`. Status transitions and their supersedes/discarded/revising cascades run synchronously from `scripts/transition_status.py` inside the invoking skill/agent — there are no status-writer hooks.

hooks.description: Record edited a4/*.md and report per-component status-consistency
Validate a4/*.md edited this session; exit 2 on violations to force fix
Delete this session's a4-edited record file
Sweep orphan a4-edited record files older than 1 day
Refresh UC implemented_by reverse-links, then report workspace-wide status-consistency
